steps.title: Spot the defining claim
steps.content: The colon reveals “there is no better example of the form”—the speaker is asserting that Kathleen Atkins Wilson's work is unsurpassed.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean surpassed or outdone — no one has topped Wilson's work as an illustrator. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — convened: means assembled or gathered — you convene a meeting, not an artist's work. Irrelevant to surpassing. ✗
  • Choice B — eclipsed: means overshadowed or surpassed — "no one has ever eclipsed her work" means it remains unmatched. ✓
  • Choice C — augmented: means added to or increased — this would mean no one has expanded her work, not that no one has surpassed it. ✗
  • Choice D — cited: means referenced — "no one has ever cited her work" contradicts the idea that it is celebrated as the best example. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
